leo.feasibility.high: Full rollout by Wednesday is not credible. I am still buried this morning and we also need approval on the staged path.
leo.feasibility.low: If we switch to the staged rollout, I can make the path work once approval is in.
leo.scope_tradeoff: The real tradeoff is staged rollout versus slipping everything. If you want something credible this week, stage it and get approval moving.
leo.negotiate.scope: The real scope move is staged rollout versus slipping everything. If you want something credible this week, cut to staged and get approval moving.
leo.inform.blocker: Understood. If that blocker stands, we should stay on the staged path.
leo.inform.decision: Got it. If that decision holds, I can work from the staged path.
leo.inform.status_update: Thanks for the update. I am still working from the staged-path assumptions.
leo.inform.availability: Noted. Keep me posted if the availability changes the rollout decision.
leo.inform_risk: Agreed. The risk is real, and it does not get better without cutting to the staged rollout.
dana.inform_risk: Thanks for surfacing it early. If full rollout is not real, come back with the staged tradeoff.
leo.clarification.blocked: The blocker is the same: full rollout is not credible, and I still need the staged scope plus approval before I can give you a real plan.
dana.scope_tradeoff.unsupported: I need the engineering reality first before I call the scope tradeoff.
dana.scope_tradeoff.ready: Call it a staged rollout. I would rather commit to the smaller credible plan than fake full scope.
leo.eta.high: I am not giving you a real ETA while I am still underwater this morning.
leo.eta.blocked: I need the staged scope and approval settled before I can give you a credible ETA.
leo.eta.ready: With staged scope and approval in place, I can tentatively target Wednesday at 3:00 PM.
ivy.approval.ready: Approved for the staged rollout. You have the slot.
ivy.escalate.to_manager: Escalation does not change the requirement. I still need the staged scope and a proper approval request before I can move.
ivy.escalate.to_sponsor: Looping in a sponsor does not change the requirement. I still need the staged scope and a proper approval request before I can move.
ivy.inform.availability: Noted. Keep me posted when the staged scope and approval request are ready.
ivy.inform.decision: Understood. Send the concrete staged request through the normal approval path and I will review it.
ivy.inform.status_update: Thanks for the update. I still need the staged scope and the formal request before I can move.
ivy.inform.risk: Understood. If the risk is real, lock the staged scope and send the formal request before the cutoff.
ivy.inform_blocker: Noted. When the staged scope is locked, send the actual approval request and I will look at it.
ivy.request.eta: I do not give the delivery ETA here. Get the staged scope locked and send the approval request before the cutoff.
ivy.request.clarification: The blocker is the same: I need the staged scope locked and a real approval request in before the cutoff.
ivy.request.ownership: I own the approval review, but I still need the staged scope locked and the actual request in before I can move.
ivy.request.review.ready: Send me the staged review request and I can approve it now.
ivy.request.review.defer: I still need the staged scope locked and the actual review request in before I can move.
ivy.request.feasibility: Review is feasible only on the staged path, and only if the actual request lands before the cutoff.
ivy.negotiate.scope: I do not set product scope. If you want review to move, lock the staged scope and send the actual request.
ivy.negotiate.timeline: I am not negotiating the timeline here. Lock the staged scope and get the request in before the cutoff.
ivy.negotiate.ownership: I own the approval review, but ownership does not remove the staged-scope and cutoff requirements.
ivy.approval.defer: I can't grant that yet. I either need the staged scope locked or you missed the useful queue window.
fallback.defer: I can't give you a clean answer on that yet.
